segments.p1-2-g1: This is an impersonal experiencer construction: мне (dative) marks the person who feels the inclination, теперь is an adverb of time placed before the impersonal verb хочется (present), and рассказать вам is an infinitival complement with the indirect object вам (dative). The whole phrase means 'I feel like telling you' or literally 'to me now it is desired to tell to you.'
segments.p1-2-g2: A vocative address: господа is an abrupt direct form of address ('gentlemen' or 'sirs') set off by a comma; grammatically it is not the subject but the addressee of рассказать/хочется.
segments.p1-2-g3: An impersonal concessive clause: желается repeated here is impersonal 'it is desired,' and the coordinated negative clause иль не желается вам это слышать ('or whether you do not desire to hear this') uses иль (literary 'or') and the infinitive слышать. Вам is again dative as the experiencer; the parallel repetition emphasizes 'whether you like it or not.'
segments.p1-2-g4: A subordinate clause introduced by почему ('why'). Я is the nominative subject; даже и is a two-word intensifier ('even'); насекомым is in the instrumental case because сделать/сделаться (зделаться here) takes an instrumental complement to indicate what one becomes; не сумел сделаться is a past-tense, negated perfective construction meaning 'didn't manage/succeed in becoming.'
segments.p1-2-g5: Скажу is first-person singular of the perfective 'to say' and functions here as a performative/future ('I will say' or 'I say'); вам is the dative indirect object and торжественно is an adverb modifying the verb. The word order places the adverb after the object for emphasis.
segments.p1-2-g6: The complement clause introduced by что ('that') contains a past-tense main verb хотел ('wanted') with много раз ('many times') as an adverbial phrase. Сделаться is the infinitive 'to become' and takes the instrumental насекомым. The whole clause reports the content of what the speaker solemnly states.
segments.p1-2-g7: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; даже и is an emphatic marker ('even'); этого is a genitive demonstrative ('of this' / 'that') referencing the preceding idea; не удостоился is the past-tense, negated reflexive verb удостоиться meaning 'to be granted/treated to (something),' which commonly governs a genitive object. Together it means 'but I didn't even receive/wasn't even granted that.'
segments.p1-2-g8: Клянусь is first-person singular present of the imperfective verb 'to swear' used performatively ('I swear'); вам is dative and marks the addressees of the oath. The verb expresses the speaker's strong assertion.
segments.p1-2-g9: Another vocative: господа addresses the listeners ('gentlemen'/'sirs'), set off by commas and not functioning syntactically inside the following clause.
segments.p1-2-g10: что introduces the content clause: слишком сознавать is an infinitival nominal phrase ('to be overly conscious' or 'being too aware'), where слишком is the adverb 'too/excessively.' The dash equates that phrase with это болезнь ('this is a disease'), making an emphatic definitional statement; болезнь is used figuratively as a malady.
segments.p1-2-g11: настоящая is an adjective 'real/genuine' modifying болезнь; it stands alone here as one of two appositive adjectives emphasizing the seriousness of the 'disease.' Agreement is nominative feminine to match болезнь.
segments.p1-2-g12: полная болезнь repeats the noun with полная ('complete/utter') to intensify the statement: 'a complete/absolute disease.' The adjective agrees with болезнь in gender and number; together they form a stout emphatic apposition.
segments.p1-2-g64: 'ну да' is a colloquial two-word interjection: 'ну' = 'well' (a discourse opener) and 'да' = 'yes' (an affirming particle). Together they function as a brief acknowledgment/transition introducing what follows.
segments.p1-2-g65: 'одним словом' is an idiomatic instrumental phrase literally 'with one word' used parenthetically to mean 'in a word' or 'in short'; 'словом' is the instrumental form of 'слово' and marks the summarizing function.
segments.p1-2-g66: This begins a relative clause with 'которые' (plural relative pronoun 'which/those [that]'). 'хоть и' is a concessive particle pair meaning 'although/even if', and 'все' here means 'everyone' (i.e. 'which, although everyone does'). The clause is setting up a general concession about these things.
segments.p1-2-g67: 'пожалуй' is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'one might say', softening the generalization just made; it is parenthetical and doesn't affect core grammar beyond hedging certainty.
segments.p1-2-g68: 'делают' is the 3rd person plural present of 'делать' ('to do'), agreeing with the plural relative 'которые'; used here to state a habitual/general action ('they do [them]').
segments.p1-2-g69: 'но' ('but') introduces the contrastive continuation of the relative clause, and the repeated 'которые' resumes the relative pronoun to lead into the contrasting description of those same things.
segments.p1-2-g70: 'как нарочно' is an adverbial phrase literally 'as if purposely' or 'as if on purpose'; 'как' is a comparative particle 'as/like' and 'нарочно' is an adverb 'deliberately/specially', together conveying ironic or spiteful coincidence modifying the verb that follows.
segments.p1-2-g71: 'приходились' is the past imperfect plural of 'приходиться' meaning 'befell/occurred to' or 'fell to' (here: 'came upon me'); it agrees with the plural 'которые'. 'у меня' ('with me' / 'to me') indicates the experiencer, and 'именно тогда' ('precisely then') pinpoints the timing.
segments.p1-2-g72: A temporal subordinate clause introduced by 'когда' ('when'). 'я' is the subject, 'наиболее' is a superlative adverb 'most/especially', and 'сознавал' (imperfect past of 'сознавать') means 'was aware' or 'realized' — here indicating the moments when his awareness was strongest.
segments.p1-2-g73: Content clause introduced by 'что' ('that'). 'их' ('them') is the object (accusative) referring back to the things in question; 'совсем' adds force ('at all/completely'); the particle 'бы' marks a conditional/subjunctive shading with 'надо' ('should/ought to'), and the infinitive 'делать' is the verb governed by 'надо'. The word order splits the object ('их') from its infinitive, a common Russian arrangement: 'that I really shouldn't do them.'
segments.p1-2-g74: Чем ... тем is a correlative comparative: "Чем больше...," sets up the first limb ('the more I ...') and requires the matching "тем" in the second limb. Here 'Чем' introduces the comparative clause; 'больше' is the comparative 'more'. 'я сознавал' is imperfective past, meaning a repeated/continuing awareness ('I was becoming/more and more aware'). The preposition 'о' with 'добре' and 'о всем этом' governs the prepositional phrase 'about goodness and about all this "beautiful and lofty"'; the adjectives 'прекрасном' and 'высоком' are in the prepositional case because of 'о'.
segments.p1-2-g75: The matching 'тем' continues the correlative: 'тем глубже я и опускался...' means 'the deeper I sank...'. 'опускался' is imperfective past (repeated/progressive sinking). 'в мою тину' uses accusative after 'в' with verb of motion/penetration (into my mire). In the second half, 'тем ... способнее был совершенно завязнуть в ней' uses the comparative adjective 'способнее' ('more able') with 'быть' + infinitive 'завязнуть' to express increasing propensity: 'was more able / more likely to get completely stuck.' 'совершенно' modifies the infinitive, and 'в ней' is a pronoun referring back to 'тину'.
segments.p1-2-g76: 'Но главная черта была в том' is a common phrasing meaning 'But the main point/feature was that...'. 'была' is past-tense 'was', and 'в том' (prepositional) points forward to a 'that'-clause introduced next.
segments.p1-2-g77: This subordinate clause begins with 'что' (that). 'все это как будто не случайно во мне было' literally places the predicate 'было' at the end for emphasis (common Russian word order): 'all this, as if, was not accidental in me.' 'как будто' = 'as if/as though', 'не случайно' = 'not by accident', and 'во мне было' = 'was in me' (i.e. present in my nature/experience).
segments.p1-2-g78: The contrastive 'а' introduces 'а как будто ему и следовало так быть.' Here 'ему' is a dative impersonal pronoun ('to it'/'to that'), used with 'следовало' (past-form of the impersonal modal 'следовать') meaning 'it ought to' or 'it seemed proper/fit'. The infinitive 'быть' closes the idea: 'as if it were meant to be so.' The particle 'и' adds slight emphasis ('even/indeed').
segments.p1-2-g79: 'Как будто это было мое самое нормальное состояние' repeats the 'as if' framing. 'было' again is past tense 'was'; 'мое самое нормальное состояние' is a superlative nominal phrase ('my most normal state') with 'самое' intensifying 'нормальное'. The whole clause says the situation felt like his normal condition.
segments.p1-2-g80: 'а отнюдь не болезнь и не порча' is a sharp negation: 'а отнюдь не' = 'and by no means/not at all'. The speaker denies that this was an illness or spoilage/corruption; the repetition 'и не' before 'порча' reinforces the 'neither...nor' sense.
segments.p1-2-g81: 'так что' introduces the result clause: 'so that/therefore'. It signals the logical consequence about to be stated.
segments.p1-2-g82: 'наконец' here is a sentence adverb meaning 'finally/eventually', marking the culmination of the preceding thought.
segments.p1-2-g83: The idiom 'у меня прошла охота (бороться...)' literally 'at me the desire passed' means 'I lost the desire (to fight...)'. 'у' with the genitive 'меня' indicates possession/experiential frame; 'охота' = 'desire' (colloquial), 'прошла' = 'passed/went away', and the infinitive 'бороться с этой порчей' names what he no longer wanted to do.
segments.p1-2-g31: Но is a coordinating particle meaning 'but' or 'however'; here it introduces a contrast or continuation of the speaker's line of thought and stands alone as a brief interjection.
segments.p1-2-g32: Господа is a vocative/plural address meaning 'gentlemen' or 'sirs' used to call the audience's attention; the comma marks the direct address.
segments.p1-2-g33: This is a rhetorical question: кто же может ... тщеславиться — 'who (then) can be vain ...'. Кто is the interrogative subject, же is an emphatic particle intensifying the question, может is the present-tense modal 'can/is able to', and тщеславиться requires the instrumental case (своими болезнями) to mark the object/content of the vanity. Своими же uses the possessive свой with же to stress 'one's own'.
segments.p1-2-g34: Да еще here functions colloquially as an intensifying connector 'and besides/what's more'; ими is the instrumental pronoun 'with/by them' picking up болезнями, and форсить is a colloquial/loan verb meaning 'to show off / to flaunt' (informal register). The whole question adds the further point of flaunting one’s illnesses.
segments.p1-2-g35: Впрочем is an adverb meaning 'however' or 'anyway', used to change or soften the line of argument; it frequently prefaces a self-correction or qualification.
segments.p1-2-g36: Что ж я? is an elliptical, colloquial question: что 'what' with the particle ж (ж/же) adding emphasis or 'after all', and я 'I'. The verb is omitted (understood: 'am I saying/doing'); the effect is 'what am I (even) doing/saying?'
segments.p1-2-g37: Все это делают is a simple clause 'everyone does this': все is the plural subject 'everyone', это is the demonstrative object 'this/it', and делают is the 3rd-person-plural present verb 'do'.
segments.p1-2-g38: Болезнями-то и тщеславятся: болезнями is instrumental plural 'with/the illnesses' marking what they are vain about; the enclitic -то after the noun (болезнями-то) is an emphatic particle that narrows or highlights the word; и here adds emphasis 'even/also'; тщеславятся is 3rd-person-plural reflexive 'are vain/boast'.
segments.p1-2-g39: А я introduces a contrast between others and the speaker: а = 'but/while', and я = 'I'. The comma signals a pause before the speaker's self-assessment.
segments.p1-2-g40: Пожалуй is a modal/adverb meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', attenuating the assertion that follows.
segments.p1-2-g41: И больше всех literally 'and more than everyone'; и serves as an added emphasis ('indeed/even'), and всех is the genitive plural 'of all/everyone' used after the comparative больше (here in the clause text) to mean 'more than anyone else'.
segments.p1-2-g42: Не будем спорить is a hortative/prohibitive first-person-plural construction meaning 'let's not argue': не negates, будем is 1st-person-plural present of быть used to form a hortative/future sense, and спорить is the infinitive 'to argue'.
segments.p1-2-g43: Мое возражение нелепо is a copular clause where мое возражение 'my objection' is the nominative subject and нелепо is a predicative adverb/adjective meaning 'is absurd' or 'is ridiculous'.
segments.p1-2-g163: Чем is an interrogative pronoun in the instrumental (literally 'by what'), used to ask for the cause or means. объясняется is the present/passive (or reflexive) form of объясняться meaning 'is explained' or 'is accounted for' (here passive/impersonal). тут is an adverb 'here' / 'at this point' specifying place or point in the narrative. наслаждение is the noun 'pleasure' (the thing being explained). The clause order places the instrumental question-word first, then the verb, then adverb and the noun as the patient.
segments.p1-2-g164: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but/however'. я is the subject pronoun 'I'. объяснюсь is the 1st person singular future (perfective) reflexive of объяснять/объясниться, literally 'I will explain myself' — the reflexive -ся marks that the speaker will give an explanation (often emphatic or defensive).
segments.p1-2-g165: Я is the subject 'I'. таки is an emphatic particle (colloquial/dated) meaning 'after all' / 'indeed', adding insistence. доведу is 1st person singular future perfective of довести 'to bring/lead to' and here forms the idiom довести до конца 'to bring to an end' = 'to finish/complete'. до is the preposition 'up to/until' governing the genitive конца 'end'.
segments.p1-2-g166: Я is the subject 'I'. и here functions as a particle meaning 'also/even' (not just the simple conjunction) emphasizing the unexpected action. перо is the direct object 'pen/quill'. затем is an adverb 'then/afterwards' marking sequence. в + руки (в — 'into', руки — plural genitive/accusative 'hands') with взял (perfective 'took') forms the phrase 'took into (one's) hands' = 'took up' or 'seized'. The word order is colloquial, placing 'и' and the object before the adverb for emphasis.
segments.p1-2-g44: «Но всё-таки» is a fixed adversative adverbial phrase meaning 'all the same' or 'nevertheless'. «Я» is the subject pronoun 'I'. «Крепко убеждён» is a predicative phrase: the short-form adjective/participle убеждён (present-state) with the adverb крепко 'firmly' means 'I am firmly convinced'.
segments.p1-2-g45: The clause begins with the conjunction «что» 'that' introducing reported content. «Не только» = 'not only'; «очень много» is an intensifier + quantifier 'very much / a great deal' and governs the genitive «сознания» because много takes the genitive.
segments.p1-2-g46: «Но даже и» is an emphatic adversative cluster meaning 'but even' or 'indeed even'. «Всякое сознание» = 'any/every consciousness' (всякое = 'any, every') and «болезнь» is a predicative noun with the copula omitted (present-tense Russian often drops 'is'), giving 'is a disease'—here used figuratively.
segments.p1-2-g47: «Я» is the subject 'I'. «Стою» is the 1st-person singular present of 'стоять' and in the idiom «стоять на (чём)» means 'to insist on' or 'stand by' something; here it is used with the prepositional «на том» ('on that' = 'that position'). «Том» is the demonstrative in the prepositional case.
segments.p1-2-g48: «Оставим» is 1st-person plural hortative/indicative ('let us leave' / 'we will leave'), here hortative: 'let's leave'. «И» adds 'also/too' or 'even', and «это» 'this/that' is the object. «На минуту» uses the preposition на with accusative to mark duration/purpose: 'for a minute'.
segments.p1-2-g49: Imperative «Скажите» ('tell'/'say') directed to the listener, with the indirect object «мне» ('to me'). «Вот» is a discourse marker introducing the forthcoming question or point, and «что» here functions as the content interrogative 'what' (introducing the clause to be asked).
segments.p1-2-g50: «Отчего» is an interrogative adverb 'why/for what reason'. «Так» 'so/thus' links to the following predicate. «Бывало» is the imperfect-past adverbial/modal use meaning 'it used to happen' or 'it would happen'—a habitual/characteristic past sense.
segments.p1-2-g51: A simple conjunction «что» 'that' linking the initial interrogative frame to the long subordinate clause described next.
segments.p1-2-g52: Adverbial phrase «как нарочно» is idiomatic: 'as if on purpose' or 'as though deliberately'—used to mark apparent deliberate coincidence.
segments.p1-2-g53: Deictic phrase «в те самые» = 'in those very (ones)' with «в» + demonstrative plural «те» + emphasizer «самые» to single out particular moments.
segments.p1-2-g54: Interjection «да» here is a confirming particle 'yes' or 'indeed', used mid-sentence to stress or correct what follows ('—yes, in those very ...').
segments.p1-2-g55: Repetition and strengthening: «в те же самые минуты» literally 'in those very same minutes'—«же» is an emphatic particle reinforcing identity with previously mentioned moments; «минуты» here = 'moments'.
segments.p1-2-g56: Relative clause: «в которые» = 'in which' (referring to the minutes). «Я наиболее способен был сознавать» uses the modal/adjectival phrase «способен был» + infinitive «сознавать» to mean 'I was most able/capable of perceiving/being aware of'. «Все тонкости» is the direct object 'all the subtleties' and «всего прекрасного и высокого» is genitive ('of all that is beautiful and lofty'); note the imperfective «сознавать» indicates ongoing/capacity rather than a single completed act.
segments.p1-2-g57: «Как говорили у нас когда-то» = 'as (they) used to say among us once' or 'as we used to say'. «Говорили» is past plural 'said/used to say', «у нас» means 'among us', and «когда-то» ('когда' + particle 'то') is the adverbial 'once / formerly'.
segments.p1-2-g58: Impersonal/habitual construction «мне случалось» = 'it happened to me' / 'I would find that' (i.e. 'it occurred that I...'), «уже» here = 'already' (indicating a change), and «не сознавать» = 'not to be conscious/aware' (imperfective infinitive, habitual or repeated lack of awareness).
segments.p1-2-g59: «А» introduces contrast ('but/whereas'). «Делать» is the infinitive 'to do/commit'. «Такие неприглядные деянья» = 'such unattractive/unsightly (i.e. disgraceful) deeds'—такие repeats the demonstrative 'such', неприглядные is an evaluative adjective (here figuratively 'unsightly/undesirable'), and деянья (plural) is a somewhat elevated/archaic word for 'deeds'.
segments.p1-2-g60: A brief repetition «такие» 'such (as these)' used for emphasis and preparing the relative clause that follows.
segments.p1-2-g61: The relative pronoun «которые» 'which' begins a relative clause but the sentence trails off—grammatically it would introduce examples of the deeds, but here it hangs, serving a rhetorical, unfinished effect.
segments.p1-2-g191: 'Главное' is a nominalized adjective meaning 'the main thing' or 'the point'; 'же' is an emphatic particle that stresses or contrasts the topic. Together they set the topic/emphasis: 'the main thing (is)'.
segments.p1-2-g192: 'как ни' here is a concessive formula meaning 'however (you)...' or 'no matter how'; 'раскидывай' is an imperfective verb form used idiomatically in a generic or hortative sense ('spread/toss about' or 'consider in different ways') — the clause means 'however you arrange/consider it'.
segments.p1-2-g193: 'А' introduces a contrast ('but'); 'все-таки' is an adverbial phrase ('nevertheless/after all') formed from 'все' + 'таки'; 'выходит' is impersonal/quotational here meaning 'it turns out' or 'the result is'.
segments.p1-2-g194: This is the content clause introduced by 'что' ('that'). Word order is colloquial/stylistic: 'всегда я первый во всем виноват выхожу' = 'я всегда выхожу (виноват) первый во всем' — 'I turn out to be the first one guilty in everything.' 'выхожу' is 1st person present of 'выходить' used in the sense 'I turn out'.
segments.p1-2-g195: 'Что' resumes the subordinate comment ('and that'); 'всего' here functions as a superlative modifier meaning 'most' or 'of all'; 'обиднее' is the comparative (used here superlatively) of 'обидный' — 'more hurtful' / 'most hurtful'.
segments.p1-2-g196: 'Без вины' is a prepositional phrase with 'без' + genitive meaning 'without (any) guilt'; 'виноват' repeats the paradoxical predicate 'guilty'; 'и' adds emphasis ('and/even'). The phrase yields 'guilty despite having no guilt.'
segments.p1-2-g197: 'Так сказать' is a parenthetical idiom meaning 'so to speak' or 'as one might say'; grammatically it's 'так' (so/thus) plus infinitive 'сказать' used to flag a figurative remark.
segments.p1-2-g198: 'По' with the dative plural 'законам' means 'according to' or 'by the laws (of)'; 'природы' in the genitive specifies 'of nature' — together 'according to the laws of nature' or 'by natural law.'
segments.p1-2-g199: Потому is a discourse connective meaning 'therefore/for that reason' that links this remark to what went before.
segments.p1-2-g200: Во-первых is the set phrase 'firstly' (во is the prefixed preposition form used here and первых is the ordinal in genitive/declension forming the idiom), marking this as the first point in a list.
segments.p1-2-g201: Виноват is an adjectival short-form meaning 'guilty' or 'to blame' and functions predicatively (I am to blame...).
segments.p1-2-g202: Что introduces a subordinate clause 'that...' (content clause). Я is the subject 'I'. Умнее is the comparative of умный ('smarter/cleverer'); here it functions predicatively. Всех is the genitive plural 'than all/everyone,' completing the comparative construction (smarter than everyone).
segments.p1-2-g203: Которые is a relative pronoun 'who/which' referring back to 'everyone'; меня is accusative 'me' (the person being surrounded), and окружают is present tense, 3rd person plural of окружать 'surround/are around,' completing the relative clause 'who surround me.'
segments.p1-2-g204: Я 'I' is the subject; постоянно 'constantly' is an adverb of frequency. Считал is past-tense imperfective 'considered/used to consider'; себя is the reflexive pronoun marking 'myself.' Умнее всех is the comparative phrase 'smarter than everyone,' where умнее is the comparative adjective and всех is the genitive plural used after the comparative.
segments.p1-2-g205: Которые меня окружают repeats the relative clause from the previous sentence: которые 'who' (relative), меня 'me' (accusative), окружают present plural 'surround/are around.'
segments.p1-2-g206: И иногда is simply 'and sometimes,' where и is the coordinating conjunction 'and' and иногда is the adverb 'sometimes.'
segments.p1-2-g207: Поверьте is the imperative/polite 'believe (me)' used parenthetically; ли is the enclitic particle often attached to make a rhetorical question or soften the phrase ('...— would you believe it?'), here adding the flavor of 'if you please'/'would you believe it.'
segments.p1-2-g208: Даже этого совестился: даже 'even' (emphatic), этого is the demonstrative pronoun in genitive referring to 'this' (the belief/feeling mentioned), and совестился is past imperfective/reflexive 'felt ashamed' (literally 'had scruples about it'), so the whole means 'I was even ashamed of that.'
segments.p1-2-g209: По крайней мере is an idiomatic phrase 'at least' ('по' here participates in the fixed expression; крайней is the feminine/declined form of 'крайний' and мере is the dative of мера 'measure'), used as a discourse hedge to soften or limit the previous claim.
segments.p1-2-g210: Я всю жизнь смотрел как-то в сторону: я 'I' subject; всю жизнь 'all my life' (всю — accusative feminine agreeing with жизнь); смотрел is past imperfective 'looked' or 'kept looking'; как-то is an adverbial particle ('somehow'/'in some manner'); в сторону is a directional phrase 'to the side.' И никогда не мог смотреть людям прямо в глаза: и 'and'; никогда не мог is the negated past ability 'could never'; смотреть is the infinitive 'to look'; людям is dative plural 'to/at people' (here 'to people's'), прямо 'directly/straight'; в глаза is the accusative plural 'in/into the eyes' (directional), so 'could never look people straight in the eye.'
segments.p1-2-g169: The single pronoun 'Я' is the grammatical subject 'I'; the following comma marks a slight pause or topicalizing of the subject before the comment. Pronouns in Russian often appear even when context would allow omitting them, but here it is explicit.
segments.p1-2-g170: 'например' is an adverbial parenthetical meaning 'for example' or 'for instance'; it modifies the whole clause and is set off by commas in speech/writing.
segments.p1-2-g171: This is a predicative short-form adjective phrase: 'ужасно' is an intensifying adverb (colloquial) and 'самолюбив' is the short masculine form of the adjective meaning 'vain' used as the predicate. Short-form adjectives function like verbs here and follow the subject.
segments.p1-2-g172: A sequence of two short-form adjectives with the subject 'Я' omitted here (understood from previous sentence): 'мнителен' and 'обидчив' are predicative short adjectives meaning 'suspicious/prone to suspect' and 'easily offended/touchy.' The conjunction 'и' links the two qualities.
segments.p1-2-g173: 'как' introduces a simile 'like/as'; 'горбун' and 'карлик' are nouns in the nominative used figuratively to compare his temperament to those characters. The comma marks the end of the comparative phrase.
segments.p1-2-g174: 'но' is the coordinating conjunction 'but,' introducing a contrast with what preceded.
segments.p1-2-g175: Here 'право' is an interjection/colloquial particle meaning 'truly' or 'indeed' (not 'right' in the moral/legal sense). It adds emphasis before the following statement.
segments.p1-2-g176: 'Бывали' is the past tense plural (used impersonally) of 'бывать' meaning 'occurred' or 'happened' in the speaker's experience; 'со мною' ('with me') marks that these moments happened to him; 'такие минуты' = 'such moments' (accusative/nominative here as the logical subject of 'бывали').
segments.p1-2-g177: 'что' introduces a content clause ('that'/'such that'); 'если б' is the conditional particle combination ('if' + conditional 'бы') and 'случилось' is past neuter of 'случиться' — here used impersonally to mean 'it had happened' (i.e., 'if it had happened'). The construction frames a hypothetical past event.
segments.p1-2-g178: Continues the subordinate conditional: the second 'что' resumes the content clause; 'мне' is the dative 'to me'; the particle 'бы' again marks the conditional; 'дали' is past plural of 'дать' used in the conditional sense 'would give' (subject omitted, general 'they/someone'); 'пощечину' is the accusative object 'a slap.'
segments.p1-2-g179: The particle 'то' (often following a conditional clause) functions as 'then' or 'in that case,' linking the 'if' clause to the result clause that follows.
segments.p1-2-g180: 'может быть' is a fixed parenthetical expression meaning 'perhaps'/'maybe'; grammatically it is a modal expression of possibility and is often set off by commas.
segments.p1-2-g181: This is the result clause in the conditional mood: 'я был бы' is the past-form + particle 'бы' combining to mean 'I would be/have been'; 'даже и этому' ('even to this') uses 'даже' for emphasis and the particle 'и' as an additional emphatic marker; 'этому' is dative 'of/for this' referring back to the slap, and 'рад' is the short-form adjective 'glad' used predicatively.
segments.p1-2-g182: Говорю is first-person singular present (я говорю) used as a prefatory remark 'I say' or 'I'm saying'. серьезно is an adverb modifying говорю, meaning 'seriously' or 'in earnest'; the colon shows this is a brief introductory comment before the main claim.
segments.p1-2-g183: наверно is a parenthetical modal adverb meaning 'probably' or 'I suppose', used to hedge the following statement; it does not change the sentence structure but signals uncertainty.
segments.p1-2-g184: This is a conditional clause with the particle бы + past tense сумел (суметь) producing 'I would be able / I could'. отыскать is the perfective infinitive complement 'to find'. и тут means 'even here' or 'also here' (и functioning as 'even'), and своего рода is a fixed genitive phrase 'of one's own sort' = 'a kind of' that modifies наслаждение, which is the direct object (accusative form identical to nominative).
segments.p1-2-g185: разумеется is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', used to mark an added clarification or emphasis; it interrupts the flow but doesn't alter the clause's syntax.
segments.p1-2-g186: наслаждение отчаяния is a noun phrase where наслаждение ('pleasure') is followed by a genitive attribute отчаяния ('of despair'), giving 'the pleasure of despair'; here it restates/specifies the 'kind of pleasure' just mentioned.
segments.p1-2-g187: но introduces contrast ('but'). в отчаянии is a prepositional phrase ('in despair'); the clitic -то on отчаянии (отчаянии-то) is a contrastive/emphatic particle that singles out 'despair' as the location/context. и before бывает works as an intensifier ('indeed/it is in… that'), and бывают (present plural of бывать) means 'occur' or 'are to be found'. самые жгучие наслаждения is a superlative noun phrase 'the keenest / most burning pleasures'.
segments.p1-2-g188: особенно когда introduces a subordinate temporal clause 'especially when…'. уж очень сильно stacks three degree adverbs (уж — a colloquial intensifier, очень — 'very', сильно — 'strongly') to amplify the verb. сознаешь is second-person singular present ('you realize' or generic 'one realizes'), and безвыходность своего положения is the accusative object phrase 'the hopelessness of one's situation' (безвыходность + genitive attribute своего положения).
segments.p1-2-g189: А is a contrastive particle ('but') introducing a turn; тут is a locative/adverbial 'here' meaning 'in this situation.' При + noun (пощечине, prepositional case) means 'on/at the occasion of' (i.e. 'when there's a slap'); the enclitic -то on пощечине gives colloquial emphasis. The dash marks a pause; да тут так и links colloquially ('and/indeed here, just...') where так и (with и as an intensifier) strengthens the following verb. Придавит is perfective simple future (3rd person singular of придавить) meaning 'will press down/crush,' and сознание о том introduces the content clause 'the awareness of the fact that...'.
segments.p1-2-g190: в какую мазь is a prepositional phrase 'into/into what kind of ointment' with какую (accusative interrogative adjective) modifying мазь (accusative object). Тебя is the direct object 'you' (accusative). Растерли is past tense, perfective, plural (they rubbed/they have rubbed) with the agent omitted; figuratively here 'растерли' means 'rubbed (you) into' or 'reduced you to' and completes the clause introduced by о том.
segments.p1-2-g13: Для introduces a purpose/beneficiary phrase ('for' or 'for the purposes of'). человеческого обихода is a genitive phrase 'of human everyday use/interaction.' слишком было бы достаточно is an impersonal hypothetical: было бы (past neuter + бы) is the conditional 'would be,' with слишком (intensifier) before the copula and достаточно ('enough') as the predicative; obыкновенного человеческого сознания is a genitive noun phrase that names what would suffice.
segments.p1-2-g14: то есть is the fixed explanatory phrase 'that is' (introducing clarification). в половину here means 'by half' / 'to the extent of a half' — an abbreviated measure of reduction that the next segment completes.
segments.p1-2-g15: в четверть меньше is a comparative measure: 'a quarter less.' меньше is the comparative 'less than,' and в четверть specifies the amount of the reduction; той порции is genitive 'of that portion,' the standard of comparison.
segments.p1-2-g16: которая is a feminine singular relative pronoun agreeing with порции ('which'). достается (доставаться) is a reflexive verb meaning 'falls to / is allotted to' (present tense here, used impersonally of what is given to someone). на долю is an idiom 'to the share/lot of' and is followed by a genitive chain that describes whose share it is: развитого человека нашего несчастного девятнадцатого столетия ('the developed man of our unhappy nineteenth century'). The conjunction и at the end links to the following participial phrase.
segments.p1-2-g17: сверх того is an adverbial phrase meaning 'besides that' / 'moreover' and introduces an additional, aggravating circumstance.
segments.p1-2-g18: имеющего is a present active participle ('having') that agrees with человека (it repeats/modifies the developed man) and is in the same case; сугубое несчастье is the object of иметь ('a particular/serious misfortune'). обитать в Петербурге is an infinitive phrase 'to dwell/live in Petersburg' that completes what the man 'has the misfortune' of doing.
segments.p1-2-g19: самом is an intensifying adjective 'the very' / 'most' used here to mark a superlative; отвлеченном и умышленном are adjectives in the prepositional agreeing with городе and describe Petersburg as 'abstract' and 'intentional/deliberate' (an ironic characterization). на всем земном шаре is an idiom meaning 'on the whole globe' / 'in the whole world.'
segments.p1-2-g20: Города is nominative plural 'cities.' бывают is the present plural/habitual form of бывать meaning 'occur / exist / are sometimes' and here conveys 'there are' or 'may be.' умышленные и неумышленные are predicate adjectives 'intentional' and 'unintentional,' classifying cities.
segments.p1-2-g21: Совершенно is an adverb 'completely' / 'perfectly' that intensifies the predicate. было бы is conditional (past neuter + бы) giving 'would be,' and довольно here functions as a predicative adverb 'enough' / 'sufficient.' Together they mean 'would be perfectly sufficient.'
segments.p1-2-g22: например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example' marking the illustrative nature of what follows.
segments.p1-2-g23: такого сознания is a genitive singular phrase 'of such consciousness' — the genitive is governed by довольно ('enough of what?'). такого is 'such,' and сознания is 'consciousness/awareness.'
segments.p1-2-g24: которым is the instrumental singular relative pronoun 'by which' agreeing with сознания (instrumental is used because жить + instrumental = 'to live by/with something'). живут is present plural 'live' (they live by that consciousness). все так называемые непосредственные люди и деятели is the plural subject 'all the so‑called immediate people and activists' — так называемые is the fixed 'so‑called' construction.
segments.p1-2-g25: Бьюсь об заклад is an idiomatic expression 'I bet / I wager.' Here бьюсь is first-person present but used idiomatically with об заклад ('on the wager').
segments.p1-2-g26: вы думаете is the direct address 'you think' (вы = you plural/formal; думаете = 2nd person plural 'think').
segments.p1-2-g27: что introduces the subordinate clause 'that.' я пишу все это is the simple clause 'I write/am writing all this' (present tense). из форсу is a colloquial prepositional phrase 'for effect / for show' (из + форс in the genitive): из = 'out of/from' and форсу denotes the notion of force/affectation here.
segments.p1-2-g28: чтоб introduces purpose ('in order that' / 'to'), and поострить is an infinitive meaning 'to make a cutting remark / to be witty/sharp.' насчет деятелей = 'about/regarding the activists/public figures' (насчёт takes the genitive).
segments.p1-2-g29: да еще = 'and moreover' / 'and what's more.' из форсу дурного тона repeats the 'for show' construction with дурного тона ('of bad tone' = in bad taste / in a bad manner). гремлю саблей is first person present 'I rattle/thunder with a saber' (гремлю = 'I thunder/rattle'; саблей is instrumental 'with a saber').
segments.p1-2-g30: как is the comparative 'like/as,' and мой офицер is 'my officer' — the speaker compares his supposed saber-rattling to that of his officer.
segments.p1-2-g141: 'А главное и конец концов' is a colloquial introductory phrase: 'А' functions as a connective ('and' / 'and so'), 'главное' is a neuter short form meaning 'the main thing', and 'и конец концов' (= 'в конце концов') is an idiomatic reinforcement meaning 'after all' or 'in the end'. Together they introduce the main point of the sentence.
segments.p1-2-g142: The conjunction 'что' introduces a subordinate clause ('that…'). 'Все это' is the subject ('all this'), and 'происходит' is present-tense, 3rd-person singular of 'происходить' meaning 'happens' or 'takes place'. The phrase 'по ... законам' uses 'по' + dative to mean 'according to' or 'by (the rules/principles of)' and the adjectives agree in dative plural with 'законам'; 'усиленного сознания' is a genitive phrase ('of intensified consciousness').
segments.p1-2-g143: 'Прямо' here means 'directly' or 'straightforwardly' and modifies the participle 'вытекающей'. 'Вытекающей' is a present active participle agreeing in case/gender/number with 'инерции' (dative feminine singular) and means 'resulting/flowing out (from)'. 'из этих законов' is the source in the genitive ('from these laws').
segments.p1-2-g144: The particle 'а' here connects clauses (roughly 'and' / 'but' in a consequential sense), and 'следственно' is an adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore', introducing the result of the preceding statement.
segments.p1-2-g145: 'Тут' (= 'here' / 'in this situation') sets the location/context. The pair 'не только не' is a fixed pattern meaning 'not only not…' (the first 'не только' = 'not only', the second 'не' negates the verb). 'Переделаешься' is future, 2nd-person singular of the reflexive verb 'переделаться' meaning 'you will remake/change yourself'; with the negation it means 'you will not change yourself.'
segments.p1-2-g146: 'Да и' is a colloquial connective meaning roughly 'and besides' or 'moreover'; 'просто' here means 'simply' or 'plainly'; 'ничего не поделаешь' is an idiom meaning 'you can't do anything (about it)' — 'поделаешь' is 2nd-person singular future of 'поделать' ('do'/'manage to do'), negated by 'не'.
segments.p1-2-g147: 'Выходит' is an impersonal/predictive present form of 'выходить' used here to mean 'it turns out' or 'the result is'. It introduces a consequence or inference from what went before.
segments.p1-2-g148: 'Например' is an adverb meaning 'for example' and signals that what follows is an illustrative case.
segments.p1-2-g149: 'Вследствие' governs the genitive and means 'due to' or 'as a consequence of'; 'усиленного сознания' is the genitive phrase 'of intensified (heightened) consciousness'.
segments.p1-2-g150: 'Прав' is the short-form adjective 'прав' (= 'right') used predicatively and referring back to some person (elliptically 'he is right'). It is masculine singular and functions as 'is right' here.
segments.p1-2-g151: 'Что подлец' is a small clause introduced by 'что' ('that') with an omitted copula — Russian often omits 'is' — so it reads 'that (he) is a scoundrel.' 'Подлец' is the predicate noun 'scoundrel'.
segments.p1-2-g152: 'Как будто' is a phrase meaning 'as if' or 'as though'. 'Это' refers to the statement just made, and 'подлецу' is dative ('to a scoundrel'), so 'как будто это подлецу утешение' literally 'as if this were a consolation to a scoundrel' — dative marks the person who would receive the consolation.
segments.p1-2-g153: 'Коль' is an archaic/conversational subordinating particle meaning 'since' or 'if' (here 'since'). 'Он уже сам ощущает' is a straightforward clause: 'he already himself feels/perceives' — 'сам' adds emphasis ('himself').
segments.p1-2-g154: 'Что' again introduces a content clause 'that he really is a scoundrel.' 'Действительно' means 'indeed/actually' and modifies the predicate noun 'подлец'.
segments.p1-2-g155: 'Но' is a simple adversative conjunction meaning 'but' or 'however'.
segments.p1-2-g158: The interjection 'Эх' (= 'эх') expresses a sigh or exclamation like 'ah' or 'oh'.
segments.p1-2-g159: 'Нагородил' is perfective past tense of a verb often used figuratively to mean 'to heave/pile up' or 'to make a muddle of (speech or deeds)'; the enclitic particle '-то' adds emphasis or contrast (colloquial).
segments.p1-2-g160: 'А' again is a connective 'but', and 'что' here is an interrogative 'what' (not the conjunction 'that'); 'объяснил' is past tense 'explained'. The whole clause asks 'but what did (you) explain?'
segments.p1-2-g211: Потому functions as a discourse connective meaning 'for that reason' or 'therefore', linking this sentence to what was just said. It's an adverb placed at the start and set off by a comma as a parenthetical discourse marker.
segments.p1-2-g212: наконец is an adverb meaning 'finally' or 'in the end', used parenthetically here to soften/qualify the upcoming admission; word order (preceding the predicate) is typical for such adverbial modifiers.
segments.p1-2-g213: виноват is the short-form masculine adjective used predicatively ('[I am] guilty' / 'to blame'); the subject 'я' is omitted but understood from context. Short-form adjectives like this serve as the main predicate and are often followed by a 'что' clause explaining the reason.
segments.p1-2-g214: This whole chunk is the subordinate clause introduced by что ('that'), giving the content of his being 'guilty'. если б ... было is a conditional clause: если + particle б (a conditional/modal particle attached to the verb) makes it subjunctive, and и here intensifies as 'even' ('если бы и было' = 'even if there were'). во мне uses the preposition во (variant of в before certain pronouns) plus the prepositional pronoun мне to mean 'in me'.
segments.p1-2-g215: то introduces the result clause of the conditional ('then'). было бы is the compound conditional ('would be' / 'there would be') with the particle бы marking irrealis. только qualifies 'only' and limits the effect to the speaker. мне же is a dative phrase 'to me' with же adding emphasis ('indeed/precisely to me'). муки больше literally 'more torments' — муки is plural noun meaning 'torments' and больше is comparative 'more' (an existential/impersonal construction: 'there would be more torment for me'). от сознания всей его бесполезности is a prepositional phrase 'from the awareness/knowledge of all its uselessness' where всей (feminine genitive singular) modifies бесполезности and его is the possessive referring back to 'великодушие'.
segments.p1-2-g216: 'Я' is the 1st-person pronoun 'I'; 'ведь' is a particle meaning 'after all' or 'you know', used to remind or justify what follows. The comma marks a pause before the speaker's qualification.
segments.p1-2-g217: 'наверно' is an adverb meaning 'probably' or 'likely' and here qualifies the whole assertion that follows as tentative.
segments.p1-2-g218: This is a conditional-negative construction: 'ничего бы не сумел сделать' uses the particle 'бы' with the past tense 'сумел' (perfective) to form a counterfactual or hypothetical ability — 'would not have been able to do anything.' 'из моего великодушия' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'out of my magnanimity' (indicating motive/source) and modifies the infinitive 'сделать'.
segments.p1-2-g219: 'ни' here is the correlative 'ni... ni...' meaning 'neither/nor', and it introduces the first infinitive 'простить' (to forgive) as one of the things he could not do.
segments.p1-2-g220: 'потому что' is the causal conjunction 'because', introducing the subordinate clause; 'обидчик' is the nominative subject of that clause meaning 'the offender' or 'the one who wronged him.'
segments.p1-2-g221: 'может' is a short parenthetical use of 'может(быть)' meaning 'may' or 'might' (indicating possibility); it modifies the following verb, softening the assertion.
segments.p1-2-g222: 'ударил' is the perfective past of 'ударить' ('struck'/'hit' or more generally 'affected'); 'меня' is the direct object 'me'; 'по законам природы' is a prepositional phrase with 'по' + dative plural meaning 'according to' or 'in accordance with' the 'laws of nature' (законам — dative plural, природы — genitive).
segments.p1-2-g223: 'а' is a contrastive conjunction 'but/and'; 'законов природы' is genitive plural 'laws of nature'; the impersonal 'нельзя прощать' ('it is impossible / one cannot forgive') states a general prohibition or impossibility — 'the laws of nature cannot be forgiven.'
segments.p1-2-g224: Another item in the 'ни... ни...' pair: 'ни' ('nor/neither') introduces the infinitive 'забыть' ('to forget') as the second thing he would not be able to do.
segments.p1-2-g225: 'потому что' again introduces a causal clause; 'хоть и' is a concessive phrase meaning 'although' or 'even though' (хоть + и together soften the concession), and 'законы природы' is the nominative plural 'laws of nature' quoted as the conceded fact.
segments.p1-2-g226: 'а' again contrasts; 'все-таки' (here written as 'все-таки') is an adverb meaning 'nevertheless' or 'still'; 'обидно' is an impersonal/neuter predicate meaning 'it is hurtful' or 'it feels offensive' — the speaker admits that, laws of nature aside, the hurt remains.
segments.p1-2-g227: Наконец is an adverbial discourse connector meaning 'finally/at last' used to sum up or introduce a concluding remark; it stands alone with a following comma.
segments.p1-2-g228: Если б (colloquial contraction of если бы) introduces a counterfactual conditional ('if...would'); even (даже) adds emphasis; the clause has я захотел быть — past tense захотел plus the infinitive быть to express a hypothetical desire to 'be' something. Вовсе here means 'completely' rather than a negating 'at all'; невеликодушным is a predicate adjective in the instrumental after быть ('to be ungenerous').
segments.p1-2-g229: А напротив is a short contrastive phrase: а is the coordinating particle 'and/but' used to mark contrast, and напротив here means 'on the contrary' (not the spatial sense). Together they introduce the opposing idea.
segments.p1-2-g230: Пожелал бы uses the past form пожелал plus the conditional particle бы to express a hypothetical 'would have wished'; отмстить is the infinitive 'to take revenge', and обидчику is the dative 'to the offender' (the person offended me), marking the target of revenge.
segments.p1-2-g231: То heads the main clause after the conditional ('then/so'); the sequence я и отмстить ни в чем никому бы не мог places the infinitive отмстить before the negated modal мог, with reinforcing particles: и adds emphasis ('even'), ни ... не is the negative construction intensified by ни (ni v chem = 'in nothing/in no respect'), в чем = 'in what' (here 'in anything'), and никому is dative 'to no one'. The conditional particle бы accompanies мог to form 'would not be able'.
segments.p1-2-g232: Потому что is the subordinating conjunction 'because' (потому 'for that reason' + что 'that') introducing the causal clause that follows.
segments.p1-2-g233: Наверно is an adverb meaning 'probably' or 'likely' and qualifies the speaker's degree of certainty about the following statement.
segments.p1-2-g234: Не решился бы is the past form решился (here 'to dare' or 'to summon courage') with negation and the conditional particle бы, giving 'would not have dared'; что-нибудь is the indefinite pronoun 'anything/something' (used with negation to mean 'anything'), and сделать is the infinitive 'to do' — 'would not have dared to do anything.'
segments.p1-2-g235: Если б даже и мог repeats the conditional formula 'if even I could': если б (if...would) + даже и (double emphasis: even) + мог (past of мочь) expressing ability in the counterfactual sense 'even if I could'.
segments.p1-2-g236: Отчего is an interrogative adverb 'why/for what reason' placed at the start of the question; не решился бы is the negative past form of решиться with conditional particle бы, here meaning 'wouldn't have dared' or 'wouldn't have made up my mind (to do it)'.
segments.p1-2-g84: Кончилось тем is an impersonal past construction: кончилось (past neuter of кончиться) = "ended up" and тем is the demonstrative used in the fixed phrase кончиться тем, что = "to end up (with the result) that..."; together they introduce the result clause that follows.
segments.p1-2-g85: что introduces a subordinate clause. я чуть не поверил is past tense with чуть не + verb = "almost believed" (чуть here = "almost"); the parenthetical a может, begins a hesitation/qualification where может (from мочь) functions idiomatically as "maybe/perhaps."
segments.p1-2-g86: и в самом деле поверил continues the parenthesis: в самом деле is an idiom meaning "indeed/really" or "in truth," and поверил is again past tense "believed," so the whole parenthesis admits that he may actually have believed it.
segments.p1-2-g87: что это begins a second that-clause embedded under поверил: что = "that," это = "this/it" and serves as the subject of the rest of the clause (that this...), connecting back to what he almost believed.
segments.p1-2-g88: пожалуй is a modal particle meaning "perhaps" or "I suppose"; it hedges the assertion that follows and softens the speaker's certainty.
segments.p1-2-g89: и есть нормальное мое состояние uses есть (present of быть) as an emphatic copula: и есть = "is indeed/actually is." Adjective + possessive + noun (нормальное мое состояние) means "my normal condition/state." Word order (adjective + possessive + noun) is natural and emphatic in Russian.
segments.p1-2-g90: А сперва-то combines the conjunction/particle а ("but/and/contrast") with сперва ("at first") and the enclitic -то, which adds emphasis or contrast ("-indeed/after all"). It's a colloquial emphatic opening: "But at first..."
segments.p1-2-g91: вначале-то repeats the temporal adverb вначале ("in the beginning") with the emphatic particle -то again; the repetition intensifies the sense of "at the very beginning" or "to begin with."
segments.p1-2-g92: сколько я муки вытерпел в этой борьбе! is an exclamatory clause: сколько ("how much/what a lot") + past tense я вытерпел ("I endured") and муки (genitive of мука) = "torments/suffering." The prepositional phrase в этой борьбе locates the suffering "in this struggle."
segments.p1-2-g93: Я не верил is simple past negative: я (I) + не верил (did not believe). It states his disbelief as a general past attitude.
segments.p1-2-g94: чтоб так бывало с другими is the content clause of не верил: чтоб (colloquial variant of чтобы) introduces a subordinate clause meaning "that"; так = "so/like that"; бывало (imperfective, impersonal/habitual) = "would happen/occur" or "ever happened"; с другими = "with/on/for others" = "to others." Together: "that this would happen to others."
segments.p1-2-g95: и потому всю жизнь таил это про себя как секрет links cause and result: и потому = "and therefore/for that reason"; всю жизнь таил = imperfective past "I hid/kept (it) all my life"; про себя = idiom "to oneself/secretly"; как секрет = "as a secret." The sequence expresses continued concealment over his life.
segments.p1-2-g96: Я стыдился (даже, is past tense "I was ashamed": я + стыдился. даже = "even" adds emphasis; the parenthesis begins an afterthought qualifying the shame.
segments.p1-2-g97: может быть is a fixed parenthetical phrase meaning "perhaps/maybe"; it's the modal qualification continued from the parenthesis.
segments.p1-2-g98: и теперь стыжусь) closes the parenthesis with a present-tense admission: и теперь = "and even now"; стыжусь = 1st-person singular present "I am ashamed (of it)."
segments.p1-2-g99: до того доходил is an idiomatic past expression: до того доходил (что...) = "I came to the point that.../it got so far that..." до того marks the degree/result and доходил is past tense "reached/came to."
segments.p1-2-g100: что ощущал какое-то тайное introduces the result clause with что = "that." ощущал is past imperfective "I felt/perceived," and какое-то тайное is the indefinite phrase "some secret..." (какое-то = "some/one of those" split here into two segments).
segments.p1-2-g101: ненормальное is an adjective "abnormal/not normal" modifying тайное; placed before the noun phrase to intensify the peculiarity of the secret feeling.
segments.p1-2-g102: подленькое наслажденьице возвращаться: a diminutive phrase. подленькое = "mean/petty," наслажденьице = diminutive "little pleasure/delight," and возвращаться is the infinitive "to return." Grammatically, the noun (наслажденьице) takes an infinitive to express the pleasure in doing that action: "a petty little pleasure in returning."
segments.p1-2-g103: бывало is a parenthetical/habitual adverb meaning "as sometimes happened" or "it would happen" — here it throws the scene back to repeated occurrences.
segments.p1-2-g104: в иную гадчайшую петербургскую ночь к себе в угол и усиленно сознавать is a complex phrase of motion and mental action: в иную гадчайшую петербургскую ночь = "on another most vile Petersburg night" (adjectives agree with ночь); к себе в угол = "to my corner" (movement toward a secluded spot); и усиленно сознавать = "and intensely to realize/acknowledge" (усиленно = adverb modifying the infinitive сознавать). The infinitive сознавать continues the sequence after возвращаться, describing the mental act that accompanies the physical return.
segments.p1-2-g105: что вот и сегодня сделал опять гадость is a subordinate clause: что = "that/which," вот (discourse particle) = "well/indeed/even," и сегодня сделал опять = "even today did again," with гадость (accusative) = "a nasty/filthy thing" as the direct object. Сделал is past tense.
segments.p1-2-g106: что сделанного опять-таки никак не воротишь is another subordinate clause explaining the irreversibility: сделанного is a nominalized past participle ("what has been done"), опять-таки = emphatic "again/after all," никак не воротишь = "you simply cannot turn back/undo it" (никак не = "in no way/can't"). Воротишь is 2nd-person present, used generically here.
segments.p1-2-g107: и внутренно, links with the ensuing series of internal actions: и = "and," внутренно = adverb "inwardly/internally," marking the mental/inner character of what follows.
segments.p1-2-g108: тайно is an adverb 'secretly' or 'privately,' reinforcing the clandestine/inner nature of the self-punishment described.
segments.p1-2-g109: грызть is an infinitive "to gnaw" used here as the first in a series of verbs describing how he attacks himself inwardly; the bare infinitive lists actions rather than forming a finite predicate.
segments.p1-2-g110: грызть себя за это зубами repeats the verb with its object and means "to gnaw at oneself for that with (one's) teeth": себя = reflexive object "oneself," за это = "for that/because of that," зубами (instrumental) = "with (his) teeth," indicating manner.
segments.p1-2-g111: пилить и сосать себя до того continues the series: пилить (here figurative "to nag/torment oneself") и сосать ("to suck") себя = two infinitives with the reflexive object; до того = "to such a degree" (preparing the result clause that follows, до того, что ...).
segments.p1-2-g112: что горечь обращалась наконец в какую-то позорную is the consequence clause: что = "that," горечь ("bitterness" as an emotion) is the subject, обращалась (imperfective past of обращаться) = "turned/was turning into," наконец = "finally," в какую-то позорную = "into some shameful (thing)."
segments.p1-2-g113: проклятую сладость и наконец - в решительное, continues the transformation: проклятую сладость = "an accursed/damned sweetness/delight" (проклятую modifies сладость), and и наконец - в решительное repeats the motion of transforming into something else: "and finally into a decisive..."; the hyphen sets off a climactic pause.
segments.p1-2-g114: серьезное наслаждение! completes the clause with the final result: серьезное = "serious/earnest," наслаждение = "pleasure/delight." The exclamation marks the emotional intensity of the confession.
segments.p1-2-g237: Об этом is the prepositional phrase 'about this' (об is the variant of о used before vowels; этом is the demonstrative pronoun это in the prepositional case). Мне хочется is an impersonal dative construction: мне (dative 'to me', the experiencer) + хочется (3rd person singular of хотелось/хотеться) meaning 'I feel like' or 'I would like'. Сказать is the infinitive governed by хочется ('to say'). Два слова is a numeral phrase: два requires the genitive form слова, here idiomatically 'a couple of words' or 'a few words/remarks'. Особо is an adverb 'especially/in particular' placed after the phrase to qualify the upcoming remark; word order is natural Russian (topic fronted, impersonal verb in the middle, adverb at the end for emphasis).
segments.p1-2-g115: Да here is an emphatic interjection meaning 'yes' or 'indeed' used to start an exclamation; it does not function as a conjunction and simply gives force to what follows.
segments.p1-2-g116: в наслаждение is a short exclamatory prepositional phrase: в + accusative (наслаждение) with the sense 'into/toward pleasure' (a movement or orientation into a state of pleasure rather than merely 'in pleasure'); the phrase stands alone as an object of the exclamation.
segments.p1-2-g117: This repeats the same prepositional phrase for emphasis; the vocative-like repetition intensifies the exclamation and keeps the same grammatical structure (в + accusative).
segments.p1-2-g118: Я is the pronoun 'I' (subject). стою is first-person singular present of 'стоять' ('I stand'), used idiomatically in the construction стоять на (чём) = 'to insist on' or 'to stand by' something. на том is the preposition на with the demonstrative pronoun том in the prepositional/demonstrative sense 'on that' (literally 'on that point'), completing the idiom.
segments.p1-2-g119: Я (I) is the subject; потому и is a short adverbial phrase 'for that reason / therefore' with и adding a slight intensifying or connective flavour; заговорил is past tense, perfective, meaning 'began to speak' or 'started talking' (first-person singular implied by context).
segments.p1-2-g120: что introduces a content clause ('that'); мне is dative 'to me' marking who experiences the desire; все in this colloquial pattern (мне всё хочется) functions adverbially — 'I really/constantly feel like' (an intensifier); хочется is the impersonal present of 'хотеться' 'to feel like/want' (here 'I feel like'); наверно is a modal adverb 'probably'; узнать is the infinitive 'to find out.'
segments.p1-2-g121: бывают is the present/habitual form of 'бывать' meaning 'occur' or 'exist (habitually)'; ли is the enclitic question particle 'whether'; у + genitive (у других) denotes possession/possession-in-people 'among others' or 'other people have'; такие наслаждения is the noun phrase 'such pleasures' (такие = 'such'). The whole forms the question 'do other people have such pleasures?'.
segments.p1-2-g122: Я (I) is the subject; вам is the dative 'to you' (indirect object); объясню is first-person singular future/perfective 'I will explain' (stated as an immediate promise to the listener).
segments.p1-2-g123: This is a full clause: наслаждение (subject) было (past 'was') тут ('here' or 'in this case') именно ('precisely') от ('from/because of') followed by the genitive phrase слишком яркого сознания своего унижения ('of an excessively vivid consciousness/awareness of one's humiliation'), i.e. the source/cause is expressed with от + genitive.
segments.p1-2-g124: оттого is a conjunctive adverb meaning 'therefore' or 'from that (fact)'; it introduces the causal subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p1-2-g125: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that'; уж is an emphatic particle 'already/indeed' used here to strengthen the statement; сам is reflexive/emphatic 'yourself' (emphasizing the subject's direct experience); чувствуешь is present 'you feel/sense' — together: 'that you yourself feel'.
segments.p1-2-g126: что introduces another clause: до + genitive (последней стены) = 'up to the last wall' and дошел is past 'you have reached' (implying a final limit). The clause states that one has come up against the last barrier.
segments.p1-2-g127: что is 'that'; и here functions as an intensifier 'even/also' or simply links clauses; скверно это is 'this is nasty/bad' — скверно is a predicate adjective/adverb meaning 'bad' or 'vile', with это as the dummy pronoun 'it/this.'
segments.p1-2-g128: но introduces contrast 'but'; что и нельзя тому иначе быть is a compressed clause: что (that) + и (even/also) + нельзя (it is impossible/cannot) + тому (dative 'to that') + иначе быть ('be otherwise') — 'but that it cannot be otherwise.'
segments.p1-2-g129: что introduces 'that'; уж again is emphatic 'already/indeed'; нет тебе выхода is the impersonal construction 'there is no way out for you' (нет + dative), asserting absence of escape.
segments.p1-2-g130: что (that) + уж (already/emphatic) + никогда не сделаешься (you will never become; сделаешься is future reflexive from 'сделаться') + другим человеком (a different person) — expresses impossibility of personal transformation.
segments.p1-2-g131: что introduces a hypothetical: если б (if — with conditional particle б) даже и оставалось еще время и вера — 'if even time and faith remained' (оставалось is imperfect 'remained/was left', время и вера are the things that might remain).
segments.p1-2-g132: чтоб (so that / in order that) introduces purpose/intent with the infinitive переделаться 'to be remade / change oneself' and во что-нибудь другое 'into something else' (во + accusative +-нибудь indefinite particle), expressing the possible object of change.
segments.p1-2-g133: то is a resumptive/conclusive particle here ('then'/'in that case'), preparing the conditional result clause that follows.
segments.p1-2-g134: наверно is a modal adverb 'probably' qualifying the speaker's estimate of what would happen in that hypothetical situation.
segments.p1-2-g135: сам (emphatic 'you yourself') + бы (conditional particle) + не захотел (past tense, 'would not have wanted') + переделываться (infinitive 'to remake oneself') — expresses that even given time/faith, the person likely would not choose to change.
segments.p1-2-g136: а is a coordinating/concessive 'and/but'; захотел бы is the conditional 'if one wanted' (захотел + бы) introducing the counterfactual 'but if he did want'.
segments.p1-2-g137: так и тут бы ничего не сделал: так 'then/in that case', и adds emphasis 'even', тут 'here/in this circumstance', бы marks conditional, ничего не сделал 'would have done nothing' — even with desire, no action would follow.
segments.p1-2-g138: потому что ('because') introduces the explanation: на самом-то деле is an idiomatic phrase 'in actual fact / in reality' with the particle-то adding emphasis; и переделываться-то repeats the infinitive with the emphatic -то to highlight the act of remaking oneself as the point under question.
segments.p1-2-g139: может быть is a frozen modal phrase 'maybe / possibly' (может = 'may/it is possible', быть = 'to be'), hedging the speaker's claim.
segments.p1-2-g140: не во что is an idiomatic short phrase meaning literally 'not into what' — functionally 'there may be nothing to change into' (не negates the existential possibility + во что 'into what').

sentences.p1-2-s1: Now I feel like telling you, gentlemen — whether you want to hear it or not — why I couldn't even become an insect.
sentences.p1-2-s2: I solemnly tell you that many times I wanted to become an insect.
sentences.p1-2-s3: But I wasn't even granted that.
sentences.p1-2-s4: I swear to you, gentlemen, that being too conscious is a disease, a real, full-blown disease.
sentences.p1-2-s19: Well yes — in a word, those things which, perhaps, everyone does, but which, as if on purpose, befell me precisely when I was most aware that I absolutely shouldn't do them at all?
sentences.p1-2-s20: The more I became aware of goodness and all that "beautiful and lofty," the deeper I sank into my mire and the more capable I was of becoming completely stuck in it.
sentences.p1-2-s21: But the main feature was that all this, as it were, was not accidental in me, but seemed as if it ought to be so.
sentences.p1-2-s22: As if this were my most normal state, and by no means an illness or corruption, so that, finally, I even lost the desire to fight this corruption.
sentences.p1-2-s9: But, gentlemen, who could be vain about his own illnesses, and even flaunt them?
sentences.p1-2-s11: — Everyone does that; they even take pride in their illnesses, and I, perhaps, more than anyone.
sentences.p1-2-s12: Let's not argue; my objection is absurd.
sentences.p1-2-s41: I'll see it through to the end after all!
sentences.p1-2-s42: Then I even took up the pen in my hands.
sentences.p1-2-s13: But all the same I am firmly convinced that not only a very great amount of consciousness, but even any consciousness is a disease.
sentences.p1-2-s15: Let's leave this aside for a minute.
sentences.p1-2-s16: Tell me this: why was it that, as if on purpose, in those very—yes, in those very same minutes when I was most capable of perceiving all the subtleties of 'everything beautiful and lofty', as we used to say, it happened that I no longer perceived but did such unsightly (undesirable) deeds, such as...
sentences.p1-2-s49: The main thing is that, however you look at it, it still turns out that I'm always the first one to be blamed for everything — and, what is most hurtful, guilty without guilt, so to speak, by the laws of nature.
sentences.p1-2-s50: So, first of all, I'm to blame for being smarter than everyone who surrounds me.
sentences.p1-2-s51: (I constantly thought myself smarter than everyone around me, and sometimes, believe it or not, I was even ashamed of that.
sentences.p1-2-s52: At least, I spent my whole life looking somewhat to the side and could never look people straight in the eye).
sentences.p1-2-s45: I, for example, am terribly vain.
sentences.p1-2-s46: I am suspicious and touchy, like a hunchback or a dwarf, but, really, there have been moments with me such that if it had happened that I had been slapped, then perhaps I would even have been glad of it.
sentences.p1-2-s47: I'm speaking seriously: probably I could even find here a kind of pleasure — of course, the pleasure of despair — but it is in despair that the keenest pleasures occur, especially when you realize very strongly the hopelessness of your situation.
sentences.p1-2-s48: But here, when there's a slap — why, the very awareness of what sort of ointment they've rubbed you into will press down on you.
sentences.p1-2-s5: For ordinary human intercourse an ordinary human consciousness would be more than sufficient — that is, half or even a quarter less than the portion allotted to the lot of the developed man of our unhappy nineteenth century and, on top of that, who has the particular misfortune of living in Petersburg, the most abstract and deliberate city on the face of the globe.
sentences.p1-2-s7: It would be perfectly sufficient, for example, the kind of consciousness by which all the so-called immediate people and activists live.
sentences.p1-2-s8: I wager you think I'm writing all this to show off, to take a swipe at the men of action, and even that I'm ostentatiously rattling my saber in bad taste like my officer.
sentences.p1-2-s31: What's most important, in the end, is that all this happens according to the normal and basic laws of intensified consciousness and by an inertia that directly follows from those laws; consequently, here you not only won't remake yourself, but simply can't do anything about it.
sentences.p1-2-s32: It turns out, for example, as a consequence of intensified consciousness: he's right that he's a scoundrel — as if that were any consolation to a scoundrel, since he already himself feels that he really is one.
sentences.p1-2-s36: Ah — you've fabricated a lot, but what have you actually explained?
sentences.p1-2-s53: So, finally, I'm to blame: if there were even magnanimity in me, it would only bring me yet more torment from the knowledge of its complete uselessness.
sentences.p1-2-s54: After all, I probably couldn't have done anything out of my magnanimity: neither forgive, because the offender might have struck me according to the laws of nature, and the laws of nature cannot be forgiven; nor forget, because even if they are laws of nature, it's still hurtful.
sentences.p1-2-s55: Finally, even if I wanted to be completely ungenerous, and, on the contrary, wished to take revenge on the offender, I still wouldn't be able to take revenge on anyone in anything, because I probably wouldn't have dared to do anything even if I could.
sentences.p1-2-s23: It ended up with my almost believing (and perhaps, in fact, believing) that this, perhaps, really is my normal state.
sentences.p1-2-s24: But at first, in the beginning, how much torment I endured in that struggle!
sentences.p1-2-s25: I did not believe that such a thing happened to others, and therefore hid it all my life to myself as a secret.
sentences.p1-2-s26: I was ashamed (even, perhaps, and am ashamed even now); it got to the point that I felt some secret, abnormal, petty little pleasure in returning, as sometimes happened, on another terribly vile Petersburg night to my corner and intensely realizing that even today I had done another filthy thing, that what had been done again could by no means be undone, and inwardly, secretly, gnawing, gnawing myself for it with my teeth, to the point of sawing and sucking myself so much that the bitterness at last turned into some shameful, accursed sweetness and finally into a resolute, serious pleasure!
sentences.p1-2-s57: I'd like to say a couple of words about this in particular.
sentences.p1-2-s29: That's why I started speaking, that I really want, perhaps, to find out: do others have such pleasures?
sentences.p1-2-s30: I'll explain to you: the pleasure here was precisely from an excessively vivid awareness of one's humiliation; from the fact that you yourself feel you have come up against the last wall; that it's ugly, but that it cannot be otherwise; that there really is no way out for you, that you will never become a different person; that even if there still remained time and faith to remake yourself into something else, you probably wouldn't want to be remade yourself; and if you did want to, even then you would do nothing, because, in reality, there may be nothing to be remade into.
